natural: smart / clever
usageContext: describing someone's intelligence

examples.text: 彼女は頭がいいです。
examples.reading: かのじょはあたまがいいです。
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa atama ga ii desu.
examples.translation: She's smart.

natural: to be hungry
usageContext: the standard everyday way to say you're hungry, almost always in the past-tense form お腹が空きました

examples.text: お腹が空きました。何か食べましょう。
examples.reading: おなかがすきました。なにかたべましょう。
examples.romaji: Onaka ga sukimashita. Nanika tabemashou.
examples.translation: I'm hungry. Let's eat something.

literal: a callus forms in the ear
natural: to be sick of hearing something (heard it so many times)
usageContext: complaining lightly about being told the same thing repeatedly
saferAlternatives: 何度も聞きました (I've heard it many times)

examples.text: その話は耳にたこができるくらい聞きました。
examples.reading: そのはなしはみみにたこができるくらいききました。
examples.romaji: Sono hanashi wa mimi ni tako ga dekiru kurai kikimashita.
examples.translation: I've heard that story so many times I'm sick of it.

literal: a hand comes out of the throat
natural: to want something desperately
usageContext: describing intense desire for something, often an object
saferAlternatives: すごく欲しい (want it a lot)

examples.text: 喉から手が出るほど欲しいです。
examples.reading: のどからてがでるほどほしいです。
examples.romaji: Nodo kara te ga deru hodo hoshii desu.
examples.translation: I want it so badly I could die for it.

natural: to worry about / mind (something)
usageContext: everyday phrase for worrying about or being bothered by something; negative imperative 気にしないで is a common reassurance

examples.text: 気にしないでください。
examples.romaji: Ki ni shinaide kudasai.
examples.translation: Please don't worry about it.

natural: to have a feeling / sense that
usageContext: softening an intuition or vague impression rather than stating it as fact

examples.text: 雨が降る気がします。
examples.reading: あめがふるきがします。
examples.romaji: Ame ga furu ki ga shimasu.
examples.translation: I have a feeling it'll rain.

natural: to be overwhelmed with emotion
usageContext: describing being moved or touched by something
saferAlternatives: 感動しました (I was moved)

examples.text: その話を聞いて胸がいっぱいになりました。
examples.reading: そのはなしをきいてむねがいっぱいになりました。
examples.romaji: Sono hanashi o kiite mune ga ippai ni narimashita.
examples.translation: Hearing that story, I was overwhelmed with emotion.

natural: to be a handful / require a lot of effort (of a person or task)
usageContext: describing a child, pet, or task that needs a lot of care/attention

examples.text: 小さい子供は手がかかりますね。
examples.reading: ちいさいこどもはてがかかりますね。
examples.romaji: Chiisai kodomo wa te ga kakarimasu ne.
examples.translation: Small children take a lot of looking after, don't they.

natural: to make the effort to go somewhere in person
usageContext: emphasizing that someone physically went somewhere, often repeatedly or despite inconvenience

examples.text: 何度もその店に足を運びました。
examples.reading: なんども そのみせに あしをはこびました。
examples.romaji: Nando mo sono mise ni ashi o hakobimashita.
examples.translation: I made the trip to that shop many times.

natural: to be crazy about / have a weakness for (usually food)
usageContext: confessing a fondness you can't resist, especially sweets or a favorite food

examples.text: 私は甘い物に目がないんです。
examples.reading: わたしはあまいものにめがないんです。
examples.romaji: Watashi wa amai mono ni me ga nain desu.
examples.translation: I have a weakness for sweets.

natural: to be well-connected / know a lot of people
usageContext: describing someone with a large social/professional network
saferAlternatives: 友達が多いです (has many friends)

examples.text: 田中さんは顔が広いです。
examples.reading: たなかさんはかおがひろいです。
examples.romaji: Tanaka-san wa kao ga hiroi desu.
examples.translation: Tanaka knows a lot of people.

natural: to be proud
usageContext: describing pride, usually a warm/positive kind (e.g. pride in a family member's achievement)
saferAlternatives: 誇りに思います (I feel proud)

examples.text: 息子が合格して、鼻が高いです。
examples.reading: むすこがごうかくして、はながたかいです。
examples.romaji: Musuko ga goukaku shite, hana ga takai desu.
examples.translation: My son passed, and I'm proud.

natural: to be tight-lipped / good at keeping secrets
usageContext: praising someone's discretion or trustworthiness with secrets

examples.text: 彼は口が堅いから、安心して話せます。
examples.reading: かれはくちがかたいから、あんしんしてはなせます。
examples.romaji: Kare wa kuchi ga katai kara, anshin shite hanasemasu.
examples.translation: He's tight-lipped, so I can talk to him without worry.

natural: to fudge a number (age, count) in one's favor
usageContext: lightly accusing someone of shaving a few years off their stated age, or similar small number-fudging
saferAlternatives: 年齢をごまかす (fudge one's age)

examples.text: ちょっと鯖を読んでいませんか。
examples.reading: ちょっとさばをよんでいませんか。
examples.romaji: Chotto saba o yonde imasen ka.
examples.translation: Aren't you fudging that number a little?

literal: would even borrow a cat's paw
natural: to be extremely busy (would take help from anyone)
usageContext: hyperbole for being swamped with work and desperate for any help at all
saferAlternatives: すごく忙しいです (extremely busy)

examples.text: 今週は猫の手も借りたいぐらい忙しいです。
examples.reading: こんしゅうはねこのてもかりたいぐらいいそがしいです。
examples.romaji: Konshuu wa neko no te mo karitai gurai isogashii desu.
examples.translation: I'm so busy this week I'd take help from a cat.

natural: even experts make mistakes
usageContext: reassuring someone (or oneself) after a skilled person slips up
saferAlternatives: 誰でも間違えます (anyone can make a mistake)

examples.text: 猿も木から落ちるって言うから、気にしないで。
examples.reading: さるもきからおちるっていうから、きにしないで。
examples.romaji: Saru mo ki kara ochiru tte iu kara, ki ni shinaide.
examples.translation: They say even monkeys fall from trees, so don't worry about it.

phrase: 塵も積もれば山となる
literal: even dust, if piled up, becomes a mountain
natural: small efforts add up to something big
whoUses: everyone, often said to encourage persistence or saving money
usageContext: encouraging steady, small effort (studying, saving, practicing) over time
saferAlternatives: 少しずつ頑張りましょう (let's keep at it little by little)

examples.text: 塵も積もれば山となるから、毎日少しずつ勉強しましょう。
examples.reading: ちりもつもればやまとなるから、まいにちすこしずつべんきょうしましょう。
examples.romaji: Chiri mo tsumoreba yama to naru kara, mainichi sukoshi zutsu benkyou shimashou.
examples.translation: Little things add up, so let's study a little every day.

natural: to kill two birds with one stone
usageContext: a four-character idiom (yojijukugo) for getting two benefits from one action — a near-exact match for the English idiom

examples.text: 運動しながら音楽を聞くのは一石二鳥です。
examples.reading: うんどうしながらおんがくをきくのはいっせきにちょうです。
examples.romaji: Undou shinagara ongaku o kiku no wa isseki nichou desu.
examples.translation: Exercising while listening to music kills two birds with one stone.

phrase: 郷に入っては郷に従え
literal: when entering a village, follow the village
natural: when in Rome, do as the Romans do
whoUses: everyone, often said to travelers or newcomers
usageContext: advising someone to adapt to local customs, especially relevant for a language learner traveling to Japan

examples.text: 郷に入っては郷に従えと言いますから、日本のマナーを覚えましょう。
examples.reading: ごうにいってはごうにしたがえといいますから、にほんのマナーをおぼえましょう。
examples.romaji: Gou ni itte wa gou ni shitagae to iimasu kara, Nihon no manaa o oboemashou.
examples.translation: As they say, when in Rome do as the Romans do — let's learn Japanese manners.

natural: self-praise / tooting your own horn
whoUses: everyone, often used to preface a boast with humility
usageContext: said before praising your own work or achievement, softening the boast — comes from the old custom of each household being proud of its homemade miso
saferAlternatives: 自分で言うのもなんですが (not to say it myself, but...)

examples.text: 手前味噌ですが、この料理は自信があります。
examples.reading: てまえみそですが、このりょうりはじしんがあります。
examples.romaji: Temae miso desu ga, kono ryouri wa jishin ga arimasu.
examples.translation: Not to toot my own horn, but I'm confident in this dish.

literal: know it a hundred times over
natural: to know something perfectly well (already)
usageContext: acknowledging that you're already fully aware of something, often before doing it anyway
saferAlternatives: よくわかっています (I understand well)

examples.text: 難しいのは百も承知です。
examples.reading: むずかしいのはひゃくもしょうちです。
examples.romaji: Muzukashii no wa hyaku mo shouchi desu.
examples.translation: I'm well aware it's difficult.
